Last updated: May 9, 2026. Covers RIVALS as of Season 12, including the current weapon meta, all game modes, and the latest ranked system changes.
What Is RIVALS? RIVALS is a fast-paced first-person shooter on Roblox developed by Nosniy Games. Since launching in April 2025, it has become one of the most-played games on the platform — regularly pulling in hundreds of thousands of concurrent players and earning nominations for 4 Innovation Awards in 2026.
Unlike many Roblox games, RIVALS is a pure PvP shooter with tight gunplay, deep movement mechanics, and a competitive ranked mode. Think of it as Roblox’s answer to games like Valorant or Apex Legends, but with its own unique identity.
Why play RIVALS?
Intense, skill-based combat with a high skill ceiling 53+ weapons across 4 loadout slots to experiment with Ranked 1v1, 2v2, and 3v3 competitive modes 34+ maps with unique layouts and verticality Cross-platform play (PC, mobile, PlayStation) Regular seasonal updates with new content Getting Started: Your First Session Default Loadout When you first spawn, you start with:
Slot Default Item Notes Primary Assault Rifle Well-rounded, great for learning Secondary Handgun Basic backup weapon Melee Fists For combo cancels and finishers Utility Grenade Quick explosive damage This starting loadout is actually solid for learning. Don’t rush to change it until you understand the core mechanics.
Essential Settings to Change Immediately Before diving into serious matches, adjust these settings:
Setting Default Recommended Why FOV 70 80-90 Wider field of view = better awareness of enemies Crosshair Dynamic (large) Small, static A smaller crosshair is more precise and less distracting Camera Sensitivity Default Adjust to comfort Too fast = shaky aim, too slow = can’t track fast targets Chat On Off (in competitive matches) Removes visual clutter during fights Start in Beginner 2v2 RIVALS offers a Beginner 2v2 mode specifically for new players. This is the best way to learn without getting destroyed by experienced opponents. Play 5-10 matches here before jumping into regular matchmaking.
Visit the Shooting Range The Shooting Range is your best friend. Use it to:
Practice aiming at stationary and moving targets Test weapon recoil patterns and bullet spread Learn the timing for slide jumps and movement techniques Experiment with different loadouts before committing coins Redeem Active Codes RIVALS regularly releases codes for free Keys and cosmetics. Here are the current active codes as of May 2026:
Code Reward FREE174 3 Keys COMMUNITY23 1 Community Wrap BONUS 1 Key BOOST 1 Key ROBLOX_RTC 5 Keys Redeem codes in the Shop menu. Keys are the primary currency for buying weapons, so don’t skip this.
Core Combat Mechanics The Four-Loadout System RIVALS doesn’t have character classes or unique heroes. Instead, you customize a 4-slot loadout:
Slot Purpose Examples Primary Main weapon — defines your playstyle Assault Rifle, Sniper, Shotgun, Permafrost Secondary Backup for close-range or when primary is on cooldown Energy Pistols, Uzi, Revolver Melee Close-quarters combat and movement tools Katana, Scythe, Knife, Chainsaw Utility Tactical items for area control and support Freeze Ray, Grenade, Smoke Grenade, Medkit Each match you can use one weapon from each slot. Choosing the right combination for the map and game mode is crucial.
Match Structure Most matches follow a first-to-5 round wins format:
Players vote on a map before each match Rounds are fast — typically 1-3 minutes each Winning rounds earns coins and ranked ELO Performance (kills, assists, wins) determines coin rewards Movement Mechanics — The Foundation of RIVALS Movement is the most important skill in RIVALS. Players who master movement will beat players with better aim every time. Here’s what you need to learn, in order:
1. Slide Jump (Learn This First) How to do it: Hold a movement key (W/A/S/D), press Ctrl to slide, then immediately press Space to jump.
Why it matters: Slide jumping maintains and increases your momentum, making you faster and harder to hit. This is the #1 mechanic every RIVALS player must master.
Practice tip: Go to the Shooting Range and practice slide jumping in a straight line. Once comfortable, practice doing it while zigzagging.
2. Strafe Constantly Never stand still during a fight. Continuously move left and right while shooting. Combined with slide jumping, this makes you an incredibly difficult target.
Key insight: Many new players stop moving while aiming. This is a death sentence in RIVALS. Practice tracking targets while strafing.
3. Slide Cancel Press Ctrl to slide, then quickly release and change direction. This lets you:
Break enemy aim tracking Quickly change direction mid-fight Appear unpredictable 4. Advanced Movement Techniques Technique How When to Use Random Jump Timing Vary when you jump during slide sequences Against experienced players who predict your rhythm Grenade Boosting Use grenade explosions to propel yourself Quick escapes and aggressive pushes Scythe Dashing Scythe melee has a unique dash ability Aggressive close-range engagements Chainsaw Speed Boost Chainsaw melee grants a speed boost Chasing down camping enemies Weapon Guide for Beginners Understanding Weapon Stats Stat What It Does Target Value Lure Speed (Fire Rate) How quickly you can fire or land shots 99%+ = maximum speed Luck Affects rare hit probability and critical chance Higher = more big plays Control Makes aiming more stable and reduces recoil 0.15+ recommended Resilience Reduces weapon handling penalties under pressure 30%+ for consistency Max Weight N/A for weapons (used for fish catching in some contexts) — Note: Lure Speed caps at 99%. A weapon with 1000% lure speed isn’t faster than one with 99% — both fire at maximum rate.
Primary Weapons — Tier Overview Weapon Tier Best For Key Strength Permafrost (Contraband) S+ Everything Currently #1 meta weapon, dominates all ranges Gunblade S Close-mid range Primary + melee Trowel B Community favorite for movement tech Fists C Starter — replace as soon as possible Utility Weapons — Tier Overview Weapon Tier Key Strength Freeze Ray S Excellent crowd control — slows/freezes enemies Medkit A Self-healing, sustain in long fights Grenade A Quick and reliable explosive damage Molotov A Area denial, great on small maps Flashbang B Blinds and disorients enemies Slot Weapon Why Primary Assault Rifle Balanced at all ranges Secondary Handgun It’s free until you earn more Melee Fists Use for combo cancels Utility Grenade Reliable explosive damage Playstyle: Play medium range, use cover, focus on learning aim and movement. Upgrade out of this loadout as soon as you earn enough Keys.
Loadout 2: Early Game (~50 Keys) Slot Weapon Why Primary Burst Rifle Accurate bursts reward good aim Secondary Handgun Still serviceable for now Melee Knife 20 Keys, fast attack speed Utility Grenade Save Keys for weapons first Playstyle: The Burst Rifle rewards disciplined aim. Focus on landing clean 3-round bursts at medium range.
Loadout 3: Mid-Game Power (~200+ Keys) Slot Weapon Why Primary Shotgun Devastating close-range damage Secondary Energy Pistols Covers medium range gaps Melee Scythe Meta mobility for aggressive pushes Utility Freeze Ray Lock down enemies before engaging Playstyle: Aggressive rush. Freeze enemies, close distance with Scythe dash, and shotgun them before they can react. The Freeze Ray + Shotgun combo is one of the most effective strategies in the game.
Loadout 4: Long-Range Dominance Slot Weapon Why Primary Heavy Sniper One-shot potential at distance Secondary Uzi Covers close-range vulnerability Melee Katana Bullet deflect for survival Utility Smoke Grenade Cover your repositioning Playstyle: Hold sightlines and pick off enemies from distance. Use smoke to escape when enemies push you, and switch to Uzi for close encounters.
Map Guide Map Voting System Before each match, players vote on which map to play. Learning the layouts of popular maps gives you a significant advantage — you’ll know sightlines, hiding spots, and flanking routes.
Map Categories Category Count Description Standard Maps 14 Regular-sized maps for 1v1 and 2v2 Big Maps 6 Larger versions required for 3v3+ matches Legacy Maps 7+ Older versions available in private servers Key Maps to Learn First Map Notes Best Strategy Arena Small, symmetrical Aggressive play, close-range weapons excel Crossroads Classic Roblox homage, 3 lanes Control the center, watch all angles Station Western-themed train station Use cover effectively, mid-range combat Graveyard Multi-floor with bell tower Vertical play, control high ground Bridge Open with limited cover Long-range weapons preferred Backrooms Tight corridors Shotguns and SMGs dominate Pro Tip: Large-scale maps are mandatory for 3v3 and larger matches. Is RIVALS free to play? A: Yes, RIVALS is completely free on Roblox. Some cosmetics cost Keys or Skin Tickets, but all weapons can be earned through gameplay.
Q: What’s the best weapon for beginners? A: The Assault Rifle (free, default) is the best starting weapon. It’s well-rounded and forgiving. Once you earn Keys, upgrade to the Burst Rifle for better accuracy.
Q: What’s the current meta in RIVALS? A: As of Season 12 (May 2026), Permafrost is the #1 meta weapon (Contraband rarity). The Freeze Ray + Shotgun combo is extremely strong for aggressive play. The Katana is meta for melee due to its bullet-deflect ability.
Q: How do I get better at movement? A: Practice the slide jump in the Shooting Range. Then practice strafing while shooting. Once you’re comfortable, learn slide cancels and random jump timing. Movement is the most important skill in RIVALS.
Q: What are Keys and how do I get them? A: Keys are a currency used to buy weapons and cosmetics. Earn them through Daily Tasks, redeeming codes, events, and the Season Pass. Always prioritize spending Keys on weapons before cosmetics.
Q: Can I play RIVALS on mobile? A: Yes, RIVALS supports cross-platform play on PC, mobile (iOS/Android), and PlayStation 4/5. Be aware that PC players may have an advantage due to mouse-and-keyboard precision.
Q: How does ranked work? A: Ranked mode offers 1v1, 2v2, and 3v3 competitive matches. You play placement matches to get an initial ELO rating, then gain or lose ELO based on match outcomes. Ranks progress from Bronze to Nemesis.
Q: What’s the hardest weapon to master? A: The Bow has the highest skill ceiling among primaries due to its arcing projectile mechanics. The Katana is also high-skill due to its bullet-deflect timing requirements.

A weapon with 50 damage at close range but heavy dropoff at 50+ studs performs very differently than one with consistent damage.

Its 2-round burst fire at 14 damage per bullet delivers devastating mid-range TTK, and the unique freeze/slow effect (up to -50% movement speed) locks down opponents, preventing escape. Even after Season 3 adjustments, it remains dominant because the movement impairment utility is unmatched among primaries. It’s the cornerstone of every competitive loadout.

It excels at mid-range where most fights happen.
Sniper — The Long-Range Specialist: 50 base damage with a x3.00 crit multiplier means headshots are instant kills. The -20% move speed penalty is the tradeoff — you’re vulnerable up close. Pair with a mobile melee like Scythe.
Burst Rifle — Budget Powerhouse: At just 25 Keys, the Burst Rifle delivers high burst damage for close-mid range fights. Note: it was nerfed in the Lucky Update (March 2026) — body damage dropped from 20 to 18. Still excellent value.
Assault Rifle — The Free Meta: The best free weapon in the game. With 12 damage at 0.10s fire rate and a generous 20/100 ammo pool, it’s reliable at all ranges. The Season 3 update actually buffed its fire rate from 0.11s to 0.10s, offsetting the damage nerf (13→12).
Flamethrower — Corridor Crusher: 85 DPS continuous damage with 25 afterburn damage and 26-stud reach. On close-quarters maps like Backrooms, this is absolutely devastating. The -5% move speed is minimal.

FAQ Q: What’s the best weapon in RIVALS right now? A: Permafrost is the undisputed #1. Its freeze/slow mechanic combined with 2-round burst fire gives it unmatched time-to-kill at mid range. It’s the cornerstone of competitive play.
Q: What’s the best weapon for beginners? A: The Assault Rifle (free, default) is the best starting weapon. It’s reliable at all ranges and forgiving for new players. Once you earn Keys, upgrade to the Burst Rifle (25 Keys) for better accuracy and burst damage.
Q: Is the Paintball Gun better than the Assault Rifle? A: At higher skill levels, yes. The Paintball Gun’s 22 damage and faster fire rate reward good tracking aim. However, the Assault Rifle’s consistency and ammo pool make it more forgiving for newer players.
Q: Which melee weapon should I buy first? A: The Knife (20 Keys) is the best value — fast attack speed, +10% move speed, and the best pure damage TTK. If you prefer aggressive play, the Scythe (~40 Keys) with its dash ability is worth the extra cost.
Q: Is the Katana really worth buying for bullet deflect? A: Absolutely. The Katana’s deflect ability can turn entire fights. Q: What’s the best utility weapon? A: Freeze Ray for crowd control — a 3-second freeze enables guaranteed follow-up kills. Molotov for area denial on small maps. Medkit for sustain in long engagements. The best choice depends on your playstyle.

Last updated: May 9, 2026. Covers Sailor Piece as of the Sea 2 update (April 2026) and the Anti-Magic update, including all islands, Devil Fruits, Haki systems, and the latest meta. Game has received major updates every 1-2 weeks.
What Is Sailor Piece? Sailor Piece is an open-world sea exploration RPG on Roblox developed by Shadowrise Devs. Released in November 2025, it has quickly become one of the most popular games on Roblox — regularly pulling in ~265K concurrent players.
Inspired by One Piece and other anime series like Jujutsu Kaisen and That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime, Sailor Piece puts you in a vast ocean world where you explore islands, fight powerful bosses, collect Devil Fruits, master Haki, and grow your character from level 0 to 16,000+.
Why play Sailor Piece?
Massive open world across two seas with 20+ islands 50+ Devil Fruits with unique abilities and movesets Deep progression system with stats, Haki, races, clans, and fighting styles Challenging boss fights with millions to billions of HP Trading system and social features Frequent major updates (every 1-2 weeks) Getting Started: Your First Session Spawn and First Steps When you first join Sailor Piece:
You spawn on Starter Island (Level 0+) Talk to the Quest NPC — your first quest is to defeat 5 Thieves Defeat the Thief Boss (Level 25) as your second quest Visit the Haki Trainer — unlock Armament Haki (press G) immediately. This is essential for combat efficiency. Redeem active codes — go to Settings (top-right corner) and enter codes for free rewards Active Codes (May 2026) Code Rewards Level Req. RAIDSOON 30x Power Shard, 40x Passive Shard, 5x Secret Chest, 100x Clan Reroll, 5x Aura Crate, 4x Cosmetic Crate 10,000 2XLUCK 2x Luck Boost 10,000 CODEFORTHERESTARTSORRY3 20x Bloodline Stone, 45x Power Shard, 55x Passive Shard + more None SPECBUFFS 10x Bloodline Stone, 30x Power Shard, 40x Passive Shard, 4x Secret Chest, 100x Clan Reroll None ANTIMAGICUPDATE Various rewards 8,500 1MLIKESWOAHHH 250,000 Cash, 400 Gems, 5x Bloodline Stone, 10x Passive Shard, 15x Clan Reroll, 50x Trait Reroll, 65x Race Reroll 12,500 TRADINGFIXEDHOPEFULLY Various rewards None Redeem codes in Settings -> Code Redemption. Even if you don’t meet the level requirement, most codes have no restriction and give valuable free rewards.
Recommended First 2 Hours Checklist Goal How Why Reach Level 500 Complete thief quests on Starter Island Unlocks Jungle Island Get a fighting style Visit fighting style NPCs Better than basic Combat Unlock Observation Haki Talk to Haki Trainer Auto-dodge ability Collect Power Shards Talk to Power Master on Lawless Island later Enables power/trait upgrades Redeem all codes Settings -> Code Redemption Free gems, shards, rerolls Visit Sailor Island The main hub island Access Fruit Dealer, Race Reroller, shops The Core Progression Loop Sailor Piece’s gameplay follows this loop:
Accept quests from NPCs on your current island Grind enemies in the area for XP and Beli (money) Level up and allocate stat points into your chosen build Defeat the island boss to progress Travel to the next island for stronger enemies and better rewards Unlock new abilities (Haki, fighting styles, Devil Fruits) Repeat until you reach the level cap and unlock Sea 2 Stat Builds — The Most Important Decision The 5 Stats Stat What It Affects Build Type Melee Punching and fighting style damage Melee build Sword Sword weapon damage Sword build Defense HP and survivability Secondary stat (all builds) Power Devil Fruit damage Fruit build Gun Gun damage Niche build The #1 Mistake: Spreading Stats Evenly Do NOT put points into all 5 stats. This is the most common beginner mistake and will leave you mediocre at everything. Pick ONE damage path and commit.
Recommended Builds Build Primary Stat Secondary Stat Avoid Best For Fruit Main Power Defense Melee, Sword, Gun Players who want to use Devil Fruits Melee Main Melee Defense Power, Sword, Gun Players who prefer fighting styles Sword Main Sword Defense Power, Melee, Gun Players who want sword combat Level Cap Progression Stage Level Cap Content Sea 1 ~12,500 13 islands, base progression Sea 2 16,000 New islands, stronger enemies, guilds Ascension Up to V Permanent stat boosts, required for Sea 2 Devil Fruits — Powers and Abilities How to Get Devil Fruits Method How It Works Cost Fruit Dealer (Sailor Island) Spin for random fruits 15,000+ Beli or 50+ Gems per spin Random Spawns Fruits occasionally spawn under trees on islands Free — check regularly Boss Drops Some bosses drop rare fruits Depends on boss Trading Trade with other players Market prices vary Fruits by Rarity Rarity Fruits Approx. Price Mythical Dragon, Fiend, Kitsune 350,000 - 400,000+ Legendary Light, Quake, Spirit 100,000 - 120,000 Epic Snow 45,000 Rare Dark 50,000 Uncommon Bomb — Common Invisible — Fruit Tier List (May 2026) Tier Fruits Why S+ Dragon, Fiend, Kitsune Mythical fruits with devastating abilities and high damage scaling S Dough, Leopard, Quake Excellent AoE damage and mobility A Bomb, Control, Spirit, Venom Strong mid-game fruits with good crowd control B Buddha, Invisible, Light, Magma Situational but viable with proper stat investment C Dark Outclassed by higher-tier fruits Fruit Awakening Fruits can be awakened for stronger movesets and higher damage. This requires specific materials found in later seas (Sea 2+). Don’t worry about awakening in your early game — focus on getting a decent fruit first.
Fruit Main Tips If you’re a Fruit main, invest ONLY in Power and Defense Never put points into Melee, Sword, or Gun if you’re using a fruit Use a fruit that matches your current level — don’t save a top-tier fruit for later Awakening dramatically increases fruit power — prioritize it once you reach Sea 2 Haki System — Core Combat Progression Haki is the most important combat system in Sailor Piece. There are three types:
1. Armament Haki (Press G) What it does: Increases physical damage output and enables you to hit Logia-type enemies (elemental enemies that are normally immune to physical attacks).
How to unlock: Talk to the Haki Trainer on Starter Island.
Why it’s essential: Without Armament Haki, you cannot damage Logia-type enemies, which appear throughout the game. Always keep it active during combat.
2. Observation Haki (Press H) What it does: Grants auto-dodge/evasion chance. When active, you’ll automatically dodge some incoming attacks.
How to unlock: Progress through islands and talk to the Haki Trainer.
Why it’s important: Auto-dodge is crucial for surviving boss fights and PvP. Combine with manual Q-dodging for maximum evasion.
3. Conqueror’s Haki (Press J) What it does: Endgame Haki that provides a massive permanent damage multiplier.
Requirements to unlock:
Defeat 500 enemies with Armament Haki active Dodge 500 hits with Observation Haki active Defeat 200 bosses Why it’s the ultimate goal: Conqueror’s Haki is the strongest Haki type. The requirements are grind-heavy but worth the effort.
Fighting Styles (Melee Specs) You start with basic Combat fighting style. There are 20+ fighting styles available, each with unique movesets, hitboxes, and damage scaling.
How to Get Fighting Styles Method How Where NPC Trainers Learn from NPCs on various islands Each island has a style trainer Cost Beli or Gems Higher-tier styles cost more Top Fighting Styles (May 2026) Style Strengths Best For Sharkman Karate Large hitboxes, high damage Melee mains Water Kung Fu Fast attacks, good mobility Aggressive players Strongest in History Massive damage scaling Endgame Anos Unique moveset Specialized play Key tip for Melee mains: Higher-tier fighting styles have larger hitboxes, more invincibility frames (i-frames), and higher damage scaling with the Melee stat. Upgrade your fighting style as soon as you can afford it.

A: Yes, Sailor Piece is completely free on Roblox. Premium currency (Gems) can be earned through gameplay or purchased with Robux.
Q: What’s the best Devil Fruit for beginners? A: For beginners, any fruit from the Rare or Epic tier (like Snow or Dark) is a good start. Don’t obsess over getting a Mythical fruit immediately — focus on leveling first. Once you have resources, aim for Dragon or Quake as your endgame fruit.
Q: What’s the best race? A: Galevorn is the best for combat (+80% HP, +75% DMG). Fishman is best for grinding/leveling (+Income, +EXP gain). Reroll at the Race Reroller NPC on Sailor Island.
Q: How do I unlock Sea 2? A: You need to reach Level 12,500+, complete Ascension V, collect all 20 Ancient Fragments (scattered across Sea 1), collect all 7 Map Pieces (boss drops), and talk to the Sea Traveler NPC on World Island.
Q: What are Haki and why do I need it? A: Haki is the core combat system. Armament Haki (G) increases damage and lets you hit Logia enemies. Observation Haki (H) gives auto-dodge. Conqueror’s Haki (J) provides a massive damage multiplier but requires grinding to unlock.
Q: How do I get gems fast? A: The fastest ways are: opening chests on every island, grinding high-level NPCs, clearing dungeons, redeeming codes (some give 100-400 gems), and defeating bosses for gem drops.
Q: What fighting style should I learn? A: Start with whatever you can afford. As you progress, aim for Sharkman Karate or Water Kung Fu for good damage and mobility. Endgame styles like Strongest in History have the highest damage scaling.
Q: How often does Sailor Piece get updates? A: Very frequently — major updates every 1-2 weeks. Recent updates include Sea 2 (April 2026) and the Anti-Magic update (April 2026). Check the official Discord or wiki for patch notes.
Q: Can I play Sailor Piece on mobile? A: Yes, Sailor Piece supports PC, Mac, iPhone, Android, Xbox One, PS4, and PS5 with cross-platform play.

What Is Sailor Piece?
